proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months distribution in this 50000-patient CRC cohort is skewed right with a positive mean and contains a non-trivial fraction of patients with pfs_months at or near 0, indicating early progression events.

analyses.code: df['pfs_months'].describe(percentiles=[.05,.5,.95]); (df['pfs_months']==0).sum()
analyses.result_summary: pfs_months: mean=4.31, median=4.21, sd=2.29, min=0.00, max=16.25; 1403 patients (2.8%) have pfs_months exactly 0. Distribution is right-skewed (skew=2.98).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous feature feature_015 (range 30-90, mean 65) is positively associated with pfs_months (longer PFS at higher values).
The continuous feature feature_019 (range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is positively associated with pfs_months.
The continuous feature feature_025 (range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
The continuous feature feature_005 (range 0.02-777, mean 9.2) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.spearmanr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 strongly positively correlated with pfs_months: Spearman rho=+0.785. Mean PFS by feature_015 bin: 30-40=0.30, 40-50=1.17, 50-60=2.65, 60-70=4.29, 70-80=5.95, 80-90=7.78. Univariate OLS slope = +0.1765/unit.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.spearmanr(df['feature_019'], df['pfs_months']); OLS
analyses.result_summary: feature_019 positively associated with PFS: Spearman rho=+0.103, OLS slope=+0.465 months per unit, p<1e-100.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.spearmanr(df['feature_025'], df['pfs_months']); OLS
analyses.result_summary: feature_025 negatively associated with PFS: Spearman rho=-0.120, OLS slope=-0.073 months per unit, p<1e-150.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.spearmanr(df['feature_005'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_005 modestly negatively associated with PFS: Spearman rho=-0.047, p=1.3e-25. Effect appears small in univariate analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_014=1 (prevalence 55%) have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_014=0.
Patients with feature_023=1 (prevalence 42%) have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_023=0.
Patients with feature_006=1 (prevalence 35%) have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_006=0.
Patients with feature_027=1 (prevalence 20%) have longer pfs_months than those with feature_027=0.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_014']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_014']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_014=1 -> 3.703 months; feature_014=0 -> 5.055 months. Difference = -1.352 months. Welch's t-test p<<1e-300.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_023']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_023']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_023=1 -> 4.121; feature_023=0 -> 4.448. Difference = -0.327 months, p=1.9e-58.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_006']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_006']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_006=1 -> 4.114; feature_006=0 -> 4.419. Difference = -0.305 months, p=1.0e-49.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_027']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_027']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_027=1 -> 5.089; feature_027=0 -> 4.117. Difference = +0.972 months, p=1.7e-216. feature_027 is the only common binary with a sizeable POSITIVE effect on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 3-level ordinal feature_001 (levels 0,1,2 with frequencies 35%,50%,15%) shows a monotone dose-response relationship with pfs_months: higher level -> shorter PFS.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_001')['pfs_months'].mean(); scipy.stats.spearmanr
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_001: level 0 -> 5.27; level 1 -> 4.05; level 2 -> 2.91. Monotone decline. Spearman rho=-0.36; OLS slope per level = -1.19 months. p<<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS regression of pfs_months on all 33 features will show feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_027, feature_023, feature_006, feature_005, feature_019, feature_025 as the dominant independent predictors, while most laboratory continuous features add no independent signal.

analyses.code: statsmodels.OLS(pfs_months ~ all 33 features)
analyses.result_summary: OLS model R^2 = 0.859. Significant independent predictors with their adjusted coefficients (signed): feature_015 +0.176/unit (t=453), feature_001 -1.171/level (t=-205), feature_014 -1.362 (t=-175), feature_027 +0.943 (t=+97), feature_023 -0.354 (t=-44), feature_006 -0.287 (t=-35), feature_022 -0.357 (t=-19), feature_005 -0.005/unit (t=-21), feature_019 +0.469/unit (t=+61), feature_025 -0.076/unit (t=-76), feature_021 -0.0004/unit (t=-9). All other features non-significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 looks like a randomly assigned treatment (e.g., a drug arm) rather than a prognostic biomarker, because it is balanced across all other patient features.

analyses.code: for each feature: compare mean within feature_027==1 vs ==0; flag any standardized diff > 0.05 SD or absolute prevalence diff > 0.01.
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 is BALANCED across every other feature in the dataset: no feature shows a binary prevalence difference >0.01 between feature_027=1 and =0 arms, and no continuous feature shows a >0.05 SD difference. Strongly consistent with a randomized treatment assignment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment feature_027 produces a clinically meaningful PFS benefit (~0.97 months) overall in the full cohort.

analyses.code: y[T==1].mean() - y[T==0].mean(); ttest
analyses.result_summary: ATE of feature_027 on pfs_months in the full cohort: +0.972 months. PFS = 5.089 (treated) vs 4.117 (control). Highly significant (p=1.7e-216). Modest effect size (~22% relative improvement).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_027 is heterogeneous across binary features: at least one binary biomarker substantially modifies the effect (interaction p < 1e-10).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ T + F + T:F for each binary F; rank by interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Treatment x feature interaction screen identifies multiple highly significant modifiers: feature_023 (p=7.7e-226, beta_int=-1.63), feature_006 (p=4.1e-168, beta_int=-1.45), feature_022 (p=6.1e-23, beta_int=-1.18), feature_011 (p=1.2e-11, beta_int=+0.99). Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_027 is also modified by at least one continuous laboratory feature (interaction p < 1e-20).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ T + F + T:F for each continuous F
analyses.result_summary: Continuous interaction screen: feature_005 dominates with interaction p=8.7e-58 and beta_int=-0.025/unit. Higher feature_005 -> smaller (eventually zero) treatment benefit. Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023=1 abolishes (reduces to ~0) the PFS benefit of treatment feature_027.

analyses.code: stratified TE: TE among feature_023==1 vs ==0
analyses.result_summary: TE of feature_027 stratified by feature_023: feature_023=0 -> +1.656 months (large benefit, p<<1e-100); feature_023=1 -> +0.027 months (no benefit, p>0.1). feature_023 functions as a dichotomous resistance/non-response marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006=1 also abolishes the PFS benefit of feature_027, similar to feature_023.

analyses.code: stratified TE by feature_006
analyses.result_summary: TE of feature_027 stratified by feature_006: feature_006=0 -> +1.480 months (large benefit); feature_006=1 -> +0.026 months (no benefit). feature_006 also acts as a binary resistance marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022=1 (rare, prevalence 4.5%) also abolishes (or even slightly reverses) the PFS benefit of feature_027.

analyses.code: TE of feature_027 within feature_022==0 vs ==1
analyses.result_summary: TE of feature_027 stratified by feature_022: feature_022=0 -> +1.027 months; feature_022=1 -> -0.156 months (no benefit, slight numerical reversal). feature_022 acts as a third resistance marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 2x2 cross-classification of feature_023 and feature_006 shows that a single positive marker (either one) is sufficient to abolish the feature_027 treatment benefit; only patients negative for BOTH derive a large benefit (~2.5 months).

analyses.code: TE in each of the 4 cells of feature_023 x feature_006
analyses.result_summary: TE of feature_027 by feature_023 x feature_006: (0,0) N=18853 TE=+2.520 (p<<1e-300); (0,1) N=10249 TE=+0.071 (p=0.16); (1,0) N=13490 TE=+0.061 (p=0.17); (1,1) N=7408 TE=-0.040 (p=0.52). Confirms an OR-of-resistance pattern: any single marker positive eliminates the effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_022=0 to the joint wild-type definition (feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0) further increases the average treatment effect of feature_027 in the resulting subgroup beyond +2.52 months.

analyses.code: TE within feature_023=0 & feature_006=0 & feature_022=0
analyses.result_summary: Within the triple wild-type subgroup (N=17372, 34.7% of cohort), TE of feature_027 = +2.758 months (p<<1e-300). Adding feature_022 negativity moves TE from +2.52 to +2.76, a small additional gain. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple wild-type subgroup, the continuous feature_005 is the single dominant remaining effect modifier of feature_027, with treatment benefit concentrated at LOW values of feature_005 and disappearing at HIGH values.

analyses.code: Within WT subgroup, OLS T*F screen, then stratify by feature_005 quartile
analyses.result_summary: Within the WT (N=17372) subgroup, feature_005 has by far the largest interaction (p=7.4e-136, beta_int=-0.065/unit). TE by feature_005 quartile within WT: Q1 (<1.97) +5.03, Q2 (1.97-4.47) +5.03, Q3 (4.47-9.90) +0.68, Q4 (>9.90) -0.02. Sharp threshold around feature_005 ~= 5.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the full cohort, feature_011 (rare, prevalence 3%) appears to ENHANCE the treatment effect of feature_027 (interaction beta = +0.99). Investigate whether this is intrinsic enhancement or an artifact of feature_011 patients tending to be wild-type for the resistance markers.

analyses.code: TE within feature_011 strata; cross-tab feature_011 x feature_022 / feature_023
analyses.result_summary: Overall TE: feature_011=0 -> +0.94, feature_011=1 -> +1.93 (apparent doubling). However, feature_011 is mutually EXCLUSIVE with feature_022 (zero co-occurrence) and is anti-correlated with feature_023 (r=-0.149). Within the triple-WT subgroup, TE is +2.75 (f011=0) vs +2.91 (f011=1) - essentially identical. Conclusion: the apparent enhancement by feature_011 is largely confounded by f011=1 patients being more likely WT; intrinsic enhancement is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022, feature_011, feature_016, feature_028, feature_002 are mutually exclusive (or nearly so), suggesting a panel of alternative driver mutations / mutually exclusive molecular subtypes.

analyses.code: Sum of {feature_022, feature_011, feature_016, feature_028, feature_002} per patient, value counts; pairwise co-occurrence
analyses.result_summary: Per-patient sum across these 5 rare binary features: 0=42416 (84.8%), 1=7127 (14.3%), 2=452 (0.9%), 3=5 (0.01%). feature_022 and feature_011 have ZERO co-occurrence (perfect mutual exclusivity). Hypothesis supported - this looks like a molecular driver panel.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A composite responder subgroup defined as feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<5 will exhibit a very large feature_027 treatment effect (>4 months), while the complement will exhibit essentially no treatment effect.

analyses.code: TE within mask_resp = (f023==0)&(f006==0)&(f022==0)&(f005<5); TE within complement
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup (N=9326, 18.7% of cohort): mean PFS treated 9.10 vs control 4.09; TE = +5.013 months (p<<1e-300). Complement (N=40674): TE = +0.025 months (p=0.33). The hypothesis is strongly supported - the entire benefit of feature_027 is concentrated in this 19% subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup defined above, no further binary or continuous feature meaningfully modifies the feature_027 treatment effect (i.e., the subgroup definition is essentially complete).

analyses.code: Within responder subgroup, run T*F interaction OLS for every remaining feature
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, no feature has interaction p<0.05 (smallest p=0.073 for feature_029, beta=+0.020/unit). TE is uniform across feature_011 (+5.00 vs +5.23), feature_001 levels, feature_014 strata, and feature_015 quartiles. Subgroup definition is essentially saturated.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among prognostic predictors, feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90) is by far the strongest determinant of pfs_months in this cohort, with each 1-unit increase associated with ~0.18 additional months of PFS.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_015 (univariate); also bin means
analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS slope = +0.176 months / unit feature_015 (t=453, p<<1e-300). Mean PFS by feature_015 bin: 30-40=0.30, 40-50=1.17, 50-60=2.65, 60-70=4.29, 70-80=5.95, 80-90=7.78. Strongly monotone. The +0.176 coefficient remains essentially unchanged in the full multivariable model (+0.1758).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_015 effect on PFS is uniform across treatment arms - it is purely prognostic and does NOT modify the feature_027 treatment effect.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ T + feature_015 + T:feature_015; also TE by quartile
analyses.result_summary: TE of feature_027 by feature_015 quartile is essentially flat: Q1=+1.027, Q2=+0.893, Q3=+0.957, Q4=+0.951 months. Treatment-by-feature_015 interaction p=0.28 in the full cohort and p=0.11 within the responder subgroup. Confirmed: feature_015 is purely prognostic, not predictive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-level ordinal) is a strong prognostic factor with a monotone dose-response, but - like feature_015 - it does NOT modify the feature_027 treatment effect.

analyses.code: TE by feature_001 level; OLS interaction
analyses.result_summary: TE of feature_027 by feature_001 level: 0 -> +0.925, 1 -> +0.995, 2 -> +0.896 months. Effectively flat across levels. Interaction p=0.89. feature_001 is prognostic-only, not predictive of treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 (binary, prev 55%) is a strong negative prognostic marker (-1.36 months independent of other features), but does NOT modify the feature_027 treatment effect.

analyses.code: TE by feature_014 strata; OLS interaction
analyses.result_summary: Univariate effect of feature_014: PFS = 3.703 (=1) vs 5.055 (=0), diff = -1.352 months. Multivariable adjusted coefficient -1.362. TE of feature_027: feature_014=0 -> +0.941, feature_014=1 -> +1.011. Interaction p=0.15. feature_014 is prognostic-only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_005 (a likely tumor-burden marker) and higher feature_025 are both negative prognostic markers, but only feature_005 acts as a treatment-effect modifier - feature_025 does not.

analyses.code: Compare interaction p for feature_005 vs feature_025; check median-split TE for both
analyses.result_summary: feature_005: T*F interaction p=8.7e-58, beta_int=-0.025; median-split TE = +1.806 (low) vs +0.135 (high). feature_025: T*F interaction p=0.36, beta_int=+0.006/unit; median-split TE = +0.992 (high) vs +0.958 (low). Confirmed - feature_005 is prognostic AND predictive, feature_025 is prognostic only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL responder subgroup hypothesis: Treatment feature_027 confers a clinically meaningful PFS benefit (~+5 months) only in the subgroup defined by [feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<5]. In all other patients (~81% of cohort), the treatment provides no PFS benefit (TE ~0). The strongest single resistance modifiers, in order, are: feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, and high feature_005. Prognostic factors (feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025) influence baseline PFS but do not modify treatment effect.

Within the responder subgroup, mean PFS rises from 4.09 months (control) to 9.10 months (treated), an absolute improvement of 5.01 months and a relative improvement of 122%, while in the non-responder complement the treated and control means are within 0.03 months of each other.

analyses.code: mask_resp = (df['feature_023']==0)&(df['feature_006']==0)&(df['feature_022']==0)&(df['feature_005']<5); compare TE in responders vs complement
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup (N=9326, 18.7% of cohort): mean PFS treated = 9.101 months, control = 4.088 months, TE = +5.013 months, p<<1e-300. Complement (N=40674, 81.3%): TE = +0.025 months, p=0.33. Within the responder subgroup, no further feature shows a significant interaction with treatment (smallest p=0.073). Final responder definition: feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<5.
